intro: Kickxia hartlii, also known as Hart's calico or Hart's bugloss, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Balkans, due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Kickxia hartlii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Kickxia hartlii is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to the limited scientific research available on its safety and efficacy.
